Name: -1_students_food_housing_provide
Representative_Docs: ['if the school could apply for a grant or figure out some way to provide quality housing to students and even temporary housing for new faculty it would be a great benefit to the school. also, if there is a way to develop a cafeteria plan for students that is tied in with their tuition, students could have access to food security during their time at mesalands. faculty & staff could also benefit if they chose to.', 'increase pay to all employees, have more food pantry locations, have food pantry days for staff and faculty (there have been times i wanted to go but didn\'t want to be staff taking away from students). i don\'t think there\'s an "emergency loan" offered to staff (such as rust for students) but that would also be huge in covering unexpected payments between paychecks.', 'provide limited meal plans to all students, staff, and faculty. provide expanded meal plans and housing stipends to students, staff, and faculty who meet a need based requirement.']

Name: 1_pantry_food pantry_food_unm
Representative_Docs: ["unm's food pantry is a great option for the students, hopefully it will continue to be successful and can expand to also serve unm employees who need it. i think more advertisement/awareness of the panty is needed.", 'i think the food pantry is a great wait to address food insecurity, but i think we need to do more at cnm. i would love to see the food pantry grow to more- become a place where students, staff and faculty can go for clothing, furniture, assistance with apply for state/government assistance, legal and mental counseling, etc.', 'have a campus food pantry']

Name: 2_campus_food_food options_campuses
Representative_Docs: ['more food pantries at varied locations, perhaps a cafeteria coupon program (i think unm valencia has started doing this), maybe partner with local restaurants for reduced food options for unm students?', 'more awareness of lobopantry - i became more aware of it during the pandemic. continue raising more visibility to faculty if resources to support students. consider how the fragmentation of information across jnmâ€™s website might lead to missed opportunities to support students. consider a ux designer consult for our website.', 'ditch fast food partners and heavily subsidize healthy food or quality local food vendors on campus. subsidize a healthy grocery coop on campus for produce and make prices affordable for students.']

Representative_Docs: ['have a cafeteria', 'have a cafeteria!!!', 'have a cafeteria.']

Name: 4_faculty_pay_graduate_graduate students
Representative_Docs: ["academic advisors deserve a yearly adjustment to their pay to help them live. if you want us to preform well doing our jobs, then make sure we don't have to worry about getting to and from work, food cost, and bills. the university could also retain more advisors if they were paid a living wage", 'provide actual raises. the campus minimum was put at $15, but many people working here for nearly 10 years were given absolute shite "adjustments" when it happened and make barely over $16. employees never get more than a cost of living adjustment unless they change positions, and the cola\'s are often less than that year\'s inflation.', "if it's possible, allow the full-time staff to either opt out of the retirement funding or change the percentage taken from their bi-weekly pay to a lesser amount so people can afford to work here at unm & live a substantial life. then once those who have reached the ability to afford to continue with the retirement deductions that person can change the percentage to the initial deductions."]

Name: 10_housing_student_type_affordable
Representative_Docs: ['perhaps provide access to a campus food pantry. offer discounts at local eateries and/or grocery stores. offer an affordable meal plan on campus through the cafe.  if possible, provide an emergency staff/faculty house fund to help with utility bills and/or rent.', 'it would probably help our students if they could have help with filling in paperwork for hud housing or other low income housing on campus. also, some help in filling in any type of applications that are hard to understand for any type of low income support.', 'a campus shelter of offers of temporary or emergency housing for students. offer limited free meals or have an easy work/food-housing exchange for students- i i worked 20 hours at week at a student job ($5.00 at the time- $200 every two weeks. my rent was 300 a month...']

Name: 11_think_does_job think_good job
Representative_Docs: ['i think the university is doing a good job.', 'they have made active strides to provide for students. with the limited funding they receive from the state, it is hard to serve students and staff what they need. with grant assistance from the state, we could help solve food insecurity in rural areas such as this.', 'as usual, i wish faculty/staff were able to get more regular pay raises. i think this would help those who are in serious crises. otherwise, i think nmsu does what it can to help students and faculty/staff with the aggie cupboard and other resources.']

Name: 13_wages_graduate_lower_increase
Representative_Docs: ['increase the work-study wage and hour limits offered to students enrolled in work study programs, offer better wages for part-time and full-time staff and faculty employees. increase the housing dormitory opportunities to include students, staff, and faculty members who are married and/or have children and pets.', 'increase free or inexpensive food availability--both hot food and food pantries. increase salaries for lowest paid employees. include regular cost of living raises for employees. offer access to resources for single parents--a faculty income might be fine for one single person, but it is extremely difficult to raise a family on $55,000 a year, particularly while paying down student loans.', 'raise wages. across the board. living wages for grad students, research students, and post docs. higher wages for all staff. lower insurance costs. lower tuition costs. lower student housing costs. stop catering to "business models" and start treating the school as a public service.']

Name: 21_college_housing food_costs_think
Representative_Docs: ['i feel that housing and meal plans are priced too high. many students should experience living on campus but the pricing is so outrageous, that it is more affordable to live with family or rent a house.  campus living was so rewarding when i was in college.  i think its a great experience.', "i would like to see more funds available to assist students with housing and food. i think it is critical to the health and safety of our students. it is also critical to their ability to stay in school and get a quality education. the opportunity scholarship is very helpful, but it doesn't cover everything our students need to get through and complete a degree.", "honestly i don't think there's much the college can do to help. the issues are so much larger than many of us can address. high food prices, health costs, vehicle costs, home costs...... we can't afford to live anymore. i make 45k a year but being single with two kids i am broke every day of my life. i make too much to qualify for assistance but i can't afford to pay my bills and feed my kids."]
